purpose.summary: This program inserts child segments into an IMS database. It retrieves a root segment and then inserts a child segment related to that root. The program abends if any IMS calls fail.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: IMS PCB for the database interaction.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Root segment data to be retrieved.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Child segment data to be inserted.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph retrieves a root segment from the IMS database and then inserts a child segment. It first initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS. Then, it calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GU to retrieve the root segment PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY using ROOT-QUAL-SSA. If the PCB status is spaces, indicating a successful retrieval, it performs 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL to insert the child segment. If the root segment retrieval fails (PCB status is not spaces or 'II'), it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph uses PAUTBPCB for the PCB and handles potential errors during the root segment retrieval.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and ensures a clean exit from the paragraph.

This paragraph inserts a child segment into the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-ISRT to insert the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment using CHILD-UNQUAL-SSA. If the PCB status is spaces, indicating a successful insertion, it displays a success message. If the PCB status is 'II', it displays a message indicating the segment is already in the database. If the insertion fails (PCB status is not spaces or 'II'), it displays an error message and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph uses PAUTBPCB for the PCB and handles potential errors during the child segment insertion.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and ensures a clean exit from the paragraph.

This paragraph closes the input files INFILE1 and INFILE2. It displays a message indicating that the files are being closed. It checks the status of each file after closing and displays an error message if the close operation was not successful (WS-INFIL1-STATUS or WS-INFIL2-STATUS is not spaces or '00'). The paragraph handles potential errors during the file closing process.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and ensures a clean exit from the paragraph.
This paragraph handles program termination due to errors. It displays a message indicating that the IMS load is abending. It sets the RETURN-CODE to 16 and then terminates the program using GOBACK.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and ensures a clean exit from the paragraph.

error_handling.condition: PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II' (in 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG)
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II' (in 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL)

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of INFILE1 and INFILE2?
open_questions.context: The code closes these files, but their purpose is not evident from the provided code snippet.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the file definitions and any read operations performed on these files in other parts of the program.

open_questions.question: What is the structure and content of ROOT-QUAL-SSA and CHILD-UNQUAL-SSA?
open_questions.context: These are used in the IMS calls, but their structure is not defined in the provided code.
open_questions.suggestion: Check the copybooks or data definitions for these variables.

open_questions.question: What is the business context for this IMS load?
open_questions.context: The purpose of loading pending authorization data is unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Consult business requirements documentation or subject matter experts.
